01
01 — Brand story
MECHANICS THAT ACTUALLY CARE.
SnapFix Mechanics is a Birmingham-based automotive repair service built on one simple idea: drivers deserve honest, quick, stress-free repairs — without the rip-off feeling the industry is known for.

OUR MISSION

To deliver honest, efficient, quick repairs that get drivers back on the road fast — with no surprise costs and no cutting corners. We're open late and weekends when other garages are closed.

WHO WE'RE FOR

Anyone with a car — from daily commuters to first-time drivers. We treat every customer like we'd want to be treated ourselves.

WHAT MAKES US DIFFERENT

We stay open late and weekends. We quote fairly and stick to it. We explain what's wrong in plain English. Based in Cradley Heath and available across the West Midlands for your 4-wheel needs.

THE PROMISE

Your car fixed in just a snap. We won't waste your time, your money, or your trust.

02
02 — Logo system
THE LOGO.
The SnapFix wordmark is the core of the brand — a confident, bold display mark with "SNAP" in white, "FIX" in the signature orange, and MECHANICS in spaced-out caps.

APPROVED BACKGROUNDS

On brand blue
On white
On black
03
03 — Colour palette
THE PALETTE.
A bold, high-contrast palette. Orange drives attention, blue delivers trust, black adds edge. These three carry almost all brand work.
SNAPFIX ORANGE
Primary · Energy
HEX#FF751F RGB255, 117, 31 CMYK0, 54, 88, 0 Pantone165 C
SNAPFIX BLUE
Secondary · Trust
HEX#004AAD RGB0, 74, 173 CMYK100, 57, 0, 32 Pantone286 C
NIGHT BLACK
Accent · Weight
HEX#000000 RGB0, 0, 0 CMYK0, 0, 0, 100 PantoneBlack C
PURE WHITE
Text · Surface
HEX#FFFFFF RGB255, 255, 255 CMYK0, 0, 0, 0 PantoneWhite
LIGHT ORANGE
Support · Highlight
HEX#FFA366 RGB255, 163, 102 CMYK0, 36, 60, 0 UsageSparingly
WARM CREAM
Support · Backgrounds
HEX#FFF5E8 RGB255, 245, 232 CMYK0, 4, 9, 0 UsageNeutral

Usage ratio: Aim for roughly 60% blue, 25% orange, 10% white/black, 5% supporting colours. Orange should feel like a shout, not a backdrop.

04
04 — Typography
THE TYPE SYSTEM.
A two-tier type system: Contrail One for headings and short impactful text (mostly uppercase), and Helvetica Now Display for body copy and longer text (primarily bold weight).
Headings · Short Text Contrail One · Mostly uppercase
MECHANICS YOU CAN TRUST
Usage Headlines, taglines, short impactful phrases — primarily used in UPPERCASE
Body · Longer Text Helvetica Now Display (web: Inter) – Primarily bold weight
Honest diagnostics, fair prices, mechanics you can trust. We're open late and weekends when other garages are closed. Accidents don't care what time of day it is.
18px / Lead Your car fixed in just a snap. 16px / Body Based in Cradley Heath, serving the West Midlands.
Notes: Contrail One is primarily set in UPPERCASE for headings and short text. Helvetica Now Display should be used primarily in bold weight for body copy. Body text stays sentence case, 16px minimum on web, 10pt minimum on print.
05
05 — Voice & tone
HOW WE SOUND.
Confident without being cocky. Friendly without being fluffy. We talk like the mate who happens to know cars — not like a corporate garage manual.

BRAND PERSONALITY

Professional Trustworthy Bold Approachable Warm Minimal Direct Honest

KEYWORDS

Snap Fast Fair Honest Local Late & weekends Trusted No-nonsense

HOW WE WRITE

We say
"We're open late and weekends."
We don't say
"Our operational hours extend beyond standard industry norms."
We say
"Honest prices. No surprises."
We don't say
"Transparent pricing structure with minimal cost variance."
06
06 — Brand usage
DO'S AND DON'TS.
Keep the brand consistent and it'll do the heavy lifting. These are the rules for anyone creating content, running ads, or producing print material.
DO'S
  • Use blue as the dominant brand colour, with orange as the accent
  • Keep the logo on solid backgrounds with generous space around it
  • Write headlines in short, punchy, confident phrases
  • Always set Contrail One in UPPERCASE — it's the core typographic rule
  • Use Helvetica Now Display primarily in bold weight for maximum impact
  • Always pair "Snap" and "Fix" as one word — SnapFix, not Snap Fix
  • Lead with the phone number in any ad or flyer
  • Mention opening times (late & weekends) where relevant
DON'TS
  • Don't stretch, rotate, or recolour the logo outside approved variants
  • Don't set Contrail One in lowercase or Title Case — UPPERCASE only
  • Don't place the logo on busy photos without a solid colour panel
  • Don't mix in additional colours beyond the palette
  • Don't use decorative, script, or Comic Sans-style fonts
  • Don't use corporate jargon or overly technical language
  • Don't lowercase "SnapFix" — it's always stylised SNAPFIX or SnapFix
07
07 — Asset library
THE BRAND IN USE.
Real-world applications of the SnapFix brand — the core flyer and business card designs. Use these as templates and reference points for all future materials.

POSTER / FLYER

Poster · Front
PRIMARY PROMO FACE
A4 ✓ A3 ✓ A2 ◯
Poster · Back
REVIEWS & CONTACT
A4 ✓ A3 ✓ A2 ◯
PRINT SIZE GUIDANCE:
A4 & A3 (recommended): The poster design is natively suited to A4 (210 × 297 mm) and A3 (297 × 420 mm) — ideal for flyers, handouts, in-window display, noticeboards, and counter distribution. Type and image quality will remain crisp.

A2 (acceptable with upscaling): Will work at A2 (420 × 594 mm) for larger-format display such as shop windows or outdoor notice boards, but print the source at the highest possible resolution (300 DPI minimum for raster images). For sizes larger than A2, commission a dedicated vector re-layout to avoid any softness on photography or gradients.
